stories.title: As Pakistan seeks to broker U.S.–Iran peace, citizens watch for gains at home
stories.description: As Pakistan works to broker peace between the U.S. and Iran, many Pakistanis are watching closely to see whether the diplomatic boost will translate into improvements at home.

stories.title: What would happen next if Iran agreed to surrender its highly enriched uranium
stories.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Scott Roecker of the Nuclear Threat Initiative about how the U.S. might go about moving highly enriched uranium out of Iran.

stories.title: Trump extends U.S. ceasefire with Iran at Pakistan's request
stories.description: The extension was announced just hours before it was set to expire. The president did not provide details on how long the new ceasefire extension will last.

stories.title: U.S.-Iran ceasefire nears its end. And, Fed chair nominee faces tough hearing
stories.description: The ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is set to expire tomorrow. Peace talks between the countries remain uncertain. And, Trump's pick to lead the Federal Reserve faces a tough confirmation hearing today.

stories.title: Ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran ends soon
stories.description: The ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran ends soon. President Trump says a U.S. delegation is going to Pakistan for talks, but Iran hasn't confirmed their attendance.

stories.title: Gulf aluminum shipments stuck as Strait of Hormuz blockades put a strain on supply
stories.description: A U.S. blockade and conflict in the Strait of Hormuz have stranded Gulf aluminum shipments and driven prices to a four-year high, exposing global supply vulnerabilities and deepening trade strain.

stories.title: Trump buys time for Iran deal after frantic day of diplomacy
stories.description: The US president's decision marked the second time in as many weeks he has backed off a threat to escalate the war, buying more time

stories.title: Extension of US-Iran ceasefire gives 'breathing space'
stories.description: The BBC's chief international correspondent Lyse Doucet reports from Tehran as Trump announces an extension of the ceasefire with Iran.

stories.title: Iran sees mass redundancies from war with US and Israel
stories.description: The impact is being felt by manufacturers, retailers and the digital sector, amid warnings it could get worse if the war resumes.

stories.title: Israeli soldiers punished over vandalism of Jesus statue in Lebanon
stories.description: Two soldiers will be removed from combat duty and receive 30 days of military detention, Israel's military says.

stories.title: US intercepts and seizes Iranian-flagged cargo ship, Trump says
stories.description: Tehran vows retaliation soon for what it calls an "act of armed piracy", which comes as the US prepares for a second round of talks.

stories.title: Trump says US will not lift Hormuz blockade until deal made with Iran
stories.description: The US president's comments come amid uncertainty over whether Iran will attend peace talks in Pakistan this week.

stories.title: Tehran will never cede control of Strait of Hormuz, senior Iranian politician tells BBC
stories.description: Lyse Doucet speaks to Ebrahim Azizi, who says Iran "will decide the right of passage" through the crucial shipping route.

stories.title: French peacekeeper killed in southern Lebanon
stories.description: President Macron blames the attack on Hezbollah. The Iran-backed armed group denies "any connection" to the incident.

stories.title: A moment of risk and opportunity: How two ceasefires could boost US-Iran talks
stories.description: Some kind of diplomatic progress is now under way, but is it enough to prevent a return to war?

stories.title: UK and France to lead defensive mission in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Sir Keir Starmer has said further details of the plan will come after a meeting in London next week.

stories.title: IRGC asserting themselves as Iran 'responds to Trump’s method of conducting diplomacy in kind'
stories.description: Carys Garland is pleased to welcome Dr Burcu Ozcelik, Senior Research Fellow for Middle East Security within the Int'l Security Dept at RUSI. Dr. Ozcelik argues that Iran is simultaneously responding to external pressures: particularly the performative diplomacy of the Trump admi...

stories.title: Middle East war live: Trump says Iran 'collapsing financially' as Washington prolongs ceasefire
stories.description: President Donald Trump said on Wednesday Tehran is “collapsing financially” over the closure of the Strait of Hormuz as the US maintains its blockade of Iran’s ports. This comes after Trump announced that the US was extending its ceasefire. Follow our liveblog for the latest upda...

stories.title: Trump extends Iran ceasefire indefinitely as peace talks stall
stories.description: President Donald Trump said Tuesday the United States would indefinitely extend its ceasefire with Iran, easing fears that a fragile pause in fighting could collapse as stalled peace talks remain in limbo. The move comes amid mounting diplomatic pressure, with Pakistan scrambling...

stories.title: Trump extends ceasefire, says giving Iran time to negotiate
stories.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday extended the US ceasefire with Iran to allow more time for talks, just hours before hostilities were set to resume, but he vowed to maintain a blockade of Tehran's ports. Trump indefinitely pushed back the end of the truce, crediting a request fr...

stories.title: Ukraine reopens damaged Druzhba pipeline to unlock €90 billion EU loan
stories.description: Kyiv said Tuesday it has repaired the damaged Druzhba oil pipeline, clearing the last obstacle to a long-delayed €90 billion EU loan. The move forces Ukraine into the uncomfortable position of resuming Russian oil flows to Hungary and Slovakia even as Ukraine President Volodymyr ...

stories.title: France freezes some spending to offset €6 billion fallout from Iran war
stories.description: France says the economic fallout from the Iran war has cost France at least 6 billion euros, as the government has rolled out support measures for businesses and households, increased defence spending and faced surging borrowing costs. To offset this while meeting its budget defi...

stories.title: Was Donald Trump 'blocked' from using the nuclear codes against Iran?
stories.description: Former CIA analyst Larry Johnson alleged on a podcast this week that US President Donald Trump wanted to use the nuclear codes against Iran during an emergency White House meeting, but that he was stopped by General Dan Caine, chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff. Johnson descri...

stories.title: EU resumes aid to Ethiopia suspended over Tigray war
stories.description: In tonight's edition, the European Union is set to resume budgetary aid to Ethiopia, which was suspended when a brutal civil war broke out in 2020. Also, Nigeria’s jet fuel shortage is being driven by disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz that have curtailed global supplies. And fi...

stories.title: Iran’s UN ambassador responds to US ceasefire extension
stories.description: Iran’s UN Ambassador has responded to US President Donald Trump’s announcement of an extension to the ceasefire.

stories.title: US halts shipment of Iraq’s oil dollars in bid to curb Iran-linked groups
stories.description: A plane carrying nearly $500m in US banknotes was blocked by the US Treasury, according to The Wall Street Journal.

stories.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 54 as Trump extends ceasefire?
stories.description: Trump said the US would extend the ceasefire with Iran until it presents a proposal and talks conclude.

stories.title: Tehran held military parades as ceasefire was set to end
stories.description: Videos show large crowds attending a military parade in Tehran on Tuesday as the US-Iran ceasefire deadline approached.

stories.title: Trump extends ceasefire and hopes for ‘unified proposal’
stories.description: In a social media post, President Trump announced an extension of the ceasefire in the war on Iran.

stories.title: Iran war live: Trump says ceasefire extended as talks with Tehran in limbo
stories.description: Lebanon's disaster management unit raises death toll from weeks of Israeli attacks to 2,454, with 7,658 people injured.

stories.title: Trump remains ‘in a quandary’ despite Iran war ceasefire extension
stories.description: Barbara Slavin says Trump’s decision to extend a ceasefire with Iran is a relief, but a diplomatic solution is far off.

stories.title: US issues more Iran sanctions on eve of possible talks in Pakistan
stories.description: The Trump administration targets 14 individuals and entities over alleged links to Iran's arms industry.

stories.title: World urges restraint as US, Iran stick to guns
stories.description: • Trump eyes ‘great deal’, claims US in ‘very strong’ negotiating position• Iran’s top negotiator slams attempt to ‘turn negotiating table into table of submission’• Iranian tanker seized; Tehran says move akin to ‘state terrorism, piracy’• US Senate to vote again on resolution s...

stories.title: A year on, India has failed to substantiate allegations against Pakistan over Pahalgam attack: info minister
stories.description: Information Minister Attaullah Tarar on Wednesday said that even a year on, India has failed to provide evidence for its allegations against Pakistan over the Pahalgam attack. The April 22, 2025 attack in Pahalgam saw 26 people, mostly tourists, killed in what is being described ...

stories.title: EU divided on suspension of Israel pact as Spain pushes for action
stories.description: BRUSSELS: European countries including Spain and Ireland pushed on Tuesday to suspend a pact governing the EU’s ties with Israel but failed to garner enough support from the bloc’s other members for any action. Arriving at an EU foreign ministers’ meeting in Luxembourg, a number ...

stories.title: Macron vows to help Lebanon prepare for talks with Israel
stories.description: • Lebanese PM says country needs €500m to address humanitarian fallout• Speaker says Israel will face resistance if troops stay in Lebanon• Belgian FM terms Tel Aviv’s conduct ‘totally unacceptable’• Israel says applying diplomatic, military pressure to disarm Hezbollah PARIS/BEI...

stories.title: ‘At the request of CDF Munir, PM Shehbaz’, Trump announces extension in ceasefire until Iran submits proposal
stories.description: Key developments: Trump has announced an extension in the ceasefire with Iran until Tehran submits a “unified proposal”; however, a naval blockade of Iranian ports will remain in place; JD Vance’s visit to Pakistan ’“not happening today”, says White House official PM Shehbaz than...

stories.title: Downing Street denies exerting pressure on civil servants to approve Mandelson’s appointment as envoy to US
stories.description: Downing Street on Tuesday denied a claim from a former official that it had applied pressure on civil servants to approve the appointment of Peter Mandelson as UK envoy to Washington and seemed to dismiss security concerns. UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer remained mired in a scand...

stories.title: Iran-Israel war LIVE: Iran attacks ship in Strait of Hormuz amid Trump's ceasefire extension
stories.description: Iran has not yet responded to U.S. President Trump’s announcement of the ceasefire extension

stories.title: Iran fires on container ship in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The British military’s United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations centre said no one was hurt and there was no environmental impact from the attack

stories.title: Iran says U.S. naval blockade has little impact on food supply
stories.description: "Despite the U.S. naval blockade, we have no problem in supplying basic goods and food,” Iran’s Agriculture Minister, Gholamreza Nouri, said

stories.title: Iran hangs man convicted of links to Israel's Mossad
stories.description: "Mehdi Farid... was hanged this morning for extensive cooperation with the terrorist spy service Mossad after the case was examined and the final verdict was approved," the judiciary's Mizan Online website said.

stories.title: Hezbollah says attacked north Israel in response to Israeli truce violations
stories.description: It said the move came “in defence of Lebanon and its people and in response to the blatant” Israeli ceasefire violations, including “attacks on civilians and the destruction of their homes and villages”

stories.title: U.S. military pushes for boost in 2027 spending on drones and air defences used in Iran war
stories.description: The Pentagon plans to triple drone spending to over $74 billion and invest more than $30 billion in critical munitions, including missile interceptors, due to low stockpiles during the Iran war

stories.title: US leader gives Iran few days to return to negotiating table — news outlet
stories.description: According to the news outlet, "Trump is willing to give another three to five days of ceasefire" for Iran to decide whether it wants a deal with the US or not"

stories.title: NATO attempts to penetrate into Asia won’t save alliance from decline — Chinese expert
stories.description: Lu Chao recalled that US and Israeli strikes on Iran have widened the rift within the military bloc to an unprecedented degree

stories.title: Container ship attacked in Strait of Hormuz — UKMTO
stories.description: According to the agency, the ship’s captain reported that the vessel was allegedly approached by the Iranian Navy

stories.title: Iran still possesses significant military resources — TV channel
stories.description: US intelligence assessments state that Iran retains more than half of its Air Force assets, and more than half of its specialized Navy vessels remain intact
